overview: The NAMASTE scheme (National Action for Mechanized Sanitation Ecosystem) is a Central Sector Scheme jointly implemented by the Ministry of Social Justice & Empowerment (MoSJE), Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs (MoHUA), Ministry of Panchayati Raj (MoPR), and the Department of Drinking Water and Sanitation (DDWS), with NSKFDC as the implementing agency. It aims to ensure the safety, dignity, and social security of sanitation workers through mechanization, occupational safety training, provision of PPE, health insurance under AB-PMJAY, livelihood support, and capital subsidies for sanitation-related equipment. The scheme covers Sewer and Septic Tank Workers (SSWs), Manual Scavengers, and Waste Pickers across urban and rural India.
objectives: Ensure safety and dignity of sanitation workers in urban India and enhance their occupational safety through capacity building and improved access to PPE kits, safety devices, and machines
Bring about a behavior change amongst citizens towards sanitation workers and enhance demand for safe sanitation services
Include waste pickers in the solid waste management process by ensuring their safety and dignity
Eliminate direct contact with human faecal matter
Establish, strengthen, and capacitate Emergency Response Sanitation Units (ERSUs)
Empower sanitation workers through self-help groups and entrepreneurship, access to alternate livelihood options, and occupational safety training
eligibility: The scheme targets Sewer and Septic Tank Workers (SSWs) engaged in cleaning and maintenance of sewers including those managing pumping stations, manholes/machine holes, and sewer lines; and those engaged in septic tank cleaning. Such workers may or may not be engaged by ULB/parastatal, empanelled/registered/licensed Private Sanitation Service Organisation (PSSO) and other private sanitation service providers. Waste Pickers are defined as persons or groups of persons informally engaged in collection and recovery of reusable and recyclable solid waste from the source of waste generation the streets, bins, material recovery facilities, processing and waste disposal facilities for sale to recyclers directly or through intermediaries to earn their livelihood. The scheme applies to Waste Pickers handling municipal solid waste, including informal Waste Pickers working at streets, door to door, transfer stations, open dumpsites, landfill sites, waste disposal sites, material recovery facilities, waste recovery & recycling, repair facilities etc. and itinerant waste buyers; informal Waste sorters working at waste processing/waste recycling facilities; and informal workers/sorters engaged in this activity for a minimum period of 6 months.
benefits: The scheme provides occupational safety training, provision of personal protective equipment (PPE) kits, health insurance coverage under Ayushman Bharat-Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (AB-PMJAY), livelihood counselling and support, access to subsidized loans for the purchase of sanitation-related equipment, linkage with other government schemes, capital subsidy for procurement of sanitation-related vehicles/equipment through Swachhta Udyami Yojana (SUY), upfront capital subsidy to SSWs for sanitation-related projects (50% of project cost, up to Rs. 10.00 lakh per unit or 50% of project cost subject to a maximum of Rs. 10.00 lakh per unit), upfront capital subsidy to Private Sanitation Service Organisations (PSSOs) and Private Contractors for procurement of mechanised cleaning equipment/vehicles (25% of project cost, subject to a maximum of Rs. 10.00 lakh per unit), provision of safety devices to ULBs, workshops on hazardous cleaning of sewers and septic tanks, mechanised cleaning equipment/machines, profiling of Sewer Entry Professionals/Septic Tank Workers, extending Health Insurance Scheme Benefits, and formation of Emergency Response Sanitation Units (ERSUs).
financial_support: The scheme provides upfront capital subsidy to SSWs for procurement of sanitation-related equipment: for individual projects costing up to Rs. 15.00 lakh, 50% of the project cost; for group projects costing up to Rs. 50.00 lakh with each beneficiary's project share maximum up to Rs. 10.00 lakh. For group projects, same as above with maximum per member capital subsidy of Rs. 5 lakh and maximum group project subsidy of Rs. 25 lakh. Upfront Capital Subsidy to Private Sanitation Service Organisations (PSSOs) and Private Contractors for procurement of mechanised cleaning equipment's/vehicles: 25% of the project cost, subject to a maximum of Rs. 10.00 lakh per unit (whichever is lower). The capital subsidy is provided by way of upfront capital subsidy to SSWs. Loans are extended through Channelizing Agencies (CAs) i.e. State Channelizing Agencies (SCAs), Regional Rural Banks (RRBs) and Public Sector Banks (PSBs) to the target group under various loan based schemes such as General Term Loan (GTL) up to Rs.15.00 lacs at 3% p.a. (NSKFDC to CAs) and 6% p.a. (CAs to beneficiaries) with repayment period upto 10 years, Mahila Adhikarita Yojana (MAY) up to Rs.2.00 lacs at 2% p.a. and 5% p.a. for 5 years, Mahila Samridhi Yojana (MSY) up to Rs.1.00 lac at 1% p.a. and 4% p.a. for 3 years, Micro Credit Finance (MCF) up to Rs.1.00 lac at 2% p.a. and 5% p.a. for 3 years, Education loan (EL) up to Rs.10.00 lacs for study in India and Rs.20.00 lacs for study abroad at 1% p.a. and 4% p.a. (with 0.5% rebate for women in case of study in India) for 5 years after course completion with one year moratorium, Green Business (GB) up to Rs.2.00 lacs at 2% p.a. and 4% p.a. (with 1% rebate for women) for 6 years including moratorium, Sanitary Marts (SM) up to Rs.15.00 lacs at 2% p.a. and 4% p.a. for 10 years, Scheme for 'Pay and Use' Community Toilets up to Rs.25.00 lacs at 2% p.a. and 4% p.a. for 10 years, and Swachhta Udyami Yojana (SUY) for procurement of sanitation related equipments/vehicles up to Rs.50.00 lacs per unit at 2% p.a. and 4% p.a. (with 1% rebate for timely repayment) for 10 years after implementation period of 90 days and moratorium of 90 days.
grant_amount_per_entity: Upfront capital subsidy to SSWs: 50% of project cost, maximum Rs. 10.00 lakh per unit; for group projects: maximum per member subsidy Rs. 5 lakh, maximum group subsidy Rs. 25 lakh. Upfront Capital Subsidy to PSSOs and Private Contractors: 25% of project cost, maximum Rs. 10.00 lakh per unit.
application_process: 1. Identify and profile Sewer/Septic Tank Workers (SSWs) and Waste Pickers through profiling camps conducted by Urban Local Bodies (ULBs) in urban areas and Gram Panchayats in rural areas, with assistance of civil society organizations, cooperatives, self-help groups (SHGs), Common Service Centres (CSCs) and Waste Pickers collectives. 2. Use the NAMASTE mobile app or web portal for data collection during profiling. 3. Conduct e-KYC of Aadhaar IDs of waste pickers/SSWs for validation. 4. Submit survey data through the portal; it undergoes error flagging by Block Nodal Officer/Validators, with errors to be resolved within 7 days. 5. After validation, a unique NAMASTE ID is generated for each validated worker. 6. Block Nodal Officer distributes ID cards to all validated workers. 7. Access benefits such as PPE kits, health insurance under AB-PMJAY, occupational safety training, and capital subsidy for sanitation-related equipment through Swachhta Udyami Yojana (SUY) or direct subsidy for sanitation-related projects. 8. For capital subsidy, submit project proposal with required documents to the concerned authority for approval and disbursement.
required_documents: Any other government issued valid ID proof
deadlines: Rolling basis — profiling drives conducted in phases; continuous data updates post-verification (database to be updated annually)
implementing_agency: National Safai Karamcharis Finance & Development Corporation (NSKFDC)
caveats: Waste Pickers component under NAMASTE scheme will be implemented over a period of two financial years from 2024-25 to 2025-26
NAMASTE Scheme will be implemented over a period of three years from 2023-24 to 2025-26

The scheme does not collect any personal information (such as name, address, or contact details) when you browse the website; personal information is only used if voluntarily provided through contact form or email
Certain Portable Document Format (PDF) files are currently not accessible; information provided in the Hindi language is also not fully accessible at this time
The MSME agency/units shall be exempted from submission of Tender Fee or EMD as applicable on production of proof of current registration certification from MSME/NSIC for the tendered item
